key_insight: Molecular dynamics shows that AlCoCuFeNi high-entropy alloy thin films on Si(100) begin as small clusters, then crystallize after about 5 ns into a mixed-phase film.
engineering_relevance: The results help guide how deposition time and substrate interactions can be tuned to control phase formation and microstructure in HEA thin-film fabrication.
method: Embedded atom model for Al, Co, Cu, Fe, Ni interactions
evidence_signal: The simulation ran for 50 ns and used radial distribution functions to identify nearest-neighbor distances and estimate lattice parameters for fcc, bcc, hcp, and amorphous phases.
watch_item: The study is simulation-based and depends on chosen interatomic potentials, so real-film behavior may differ if deposition conditions or force fields are not well matched.
paper_title: Deposition and Growth of the AlCoCuFeNi High-Entropy Alloy Thin Film: Molecular Dynamics Simulation
